title: heterotheca oregona var. scaberrima
intro: Heterotheca oregona var. scaberrima, also known as bitter goldenrod, is a plant species in the sunflower family, characterized by its yellow flowers and bitter taste. Traditionally, bitter goldenrod has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and sore throats. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as a natural diuretic. Bitter goldenrod can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture for its medicinal uses, although its efficacy and safety may vary depending on individual tolerance and the specific preparation method.
